title: Fix HP preservation on character select and attack tick rate defaults

requirements.summary: Preserve character HP when backing out to character select and re-entering the dungeon, preventing it from resetting to max HP
acceptanceCriteria: Player enters a dungeon run with, e.g., 45/100 HP
Player backs out to the character select screen
The character's HP displays as 45/100, not 100/100 or 20/max
HP preservation works consistently across multiple back-and-forth navigations

file_operations.description: Before navigating from the dungeon screen back to character select, ensure the character's current HP is persisted to the backend via the setCharacterHp mutation. When re-entering the dungeon, verify the character's HP is loaded from the backend rather than being reset to max HP.

Ensure the hook correctly retrieves and uses the currentHP value from the backend character data when initializing or updating the local character state. Do not override currentHP with maxHP unless explicitly healing to full.
Display each character's currentHP (from backend) in the health bar instead of defaulting to maxHP. Ensure the health bar reflects the actual persisted HP value when a character is selected.

After combat simulation completes, save the final currentHP value to the backend using the setCharacterHp mutation before calling the completion or death callback. Ensure HP changes during combat are persisted.

requirements.summary: Set player attack tick rate to 12 ticks by default and implement enemy attack tick rate scaling (level 1 = 21 ticks, scaling down 1 tick per 5 levels)
acceptanceCriteria: Player character attacks exactly once every 12 combat ticks
A level 1 enemy attacks once every 21 ticks
A level 5 enemy attacks once every 20 ticks
A level 10 enemy attacks once every 19 ticks
Tick scaling formula is: enemyAttackTicks = 21 - Math.floor((enemyLevel - 1) / 5)
Player attack interval constant is named clearly (e.g., PLAYER_ATTACK_INTERVAL = 12) in combatEngine.ts
No other combat logic or game behavior is changed

file_operations.description: Define a named constant PLAYER_ATTACK_INTERVAL = 12 at the top of the file. Update the combat simulation loop to ensure the player attacks only once every 12 ticks (using a tick counter modulo 12). Define a function calculateEnemyAttackInterval(enemyLevel: number): number that returns 21 - Math.floor((enemyLevel - 1) / 5). For each enemy, calculate their attack interval using this function and ensure they attack only when their tick counter reaches their interval. Reset each entity's tick counter after they attack. Do not modify damage calculations, loot drops, XP formulas, or any other combat mechanics.
